title: The Old Tramp
content:
A Old Tramp slep' in our stable wunst,
     An' The Raggedy Man he caught
    An' roust him up, an' chased him off
     Clean out through our back lot!

    An' th' Old Tramp hollered back an' said, -
     "You're a purty man! - You air! -
    With a pair o' eyes like two fried eggs,
     An' a nose like a Bartlutt pear!"
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Whitcomb Riley, titled "The Old Tramp", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
